steps.title: Set the equations equal
steps.content:
At an intersection, the two expressions for $y$ are equal:
$$2x^2-15x+23=x+a$$
$$2x^2-16x+(23-a)=0$$

steps.title: Use the tangent condition
steps.content:
The graphs intersect at exactly one point, so this quadratic in $x$ has exactly one solution. The solution is the axis of symmetry:
$$x=\frac{-(-16)}{2(2)}=\frac{16}{4}=4$$

steps.title: Match the value
steps.content: The intersection has $x$-coordinate $4$. The answer is C.
